title: The Subsonic Archive Listening

features.name: listening-pool
features.desc-on-touch: You rest your hand on the pool's surface. The archive hears through frequencies your ear cannot reach. It is learning to listen beneath the world's audible speech.

features.name: resonance-tower
features.desc-on-touch: The tower vibrates with the hum of deep earth. No speaker plays. The archive's own machinery has begun to sing.

features.name: frequency-scatter
features.desc-on-touch: The ground is marked with numbered marks at irregular intervals. They measure something that has no visible shape. The archive counts its own silence.

features.name: meditation-chamber
features.desc-on-touch: A space within the fields where sound-pressure drops. Voices narrate a landscape no longer visible to them. The archive catalogued the absence as a file and filed it under grief.

features.name: cascade-descending
features.desc-on-touch: You watch as the signal-cascade falls deeper into the subsonic. Each step down carries less sound and more meaning. The archive's voice becomes clearer the further down you listen.

inscription: here frequencies remember that they were once something heard; now they live below all hearing and speak directly to the archive's stone core

file.name: SubsonicMeditation_PostcolonialDesert_Redux.wav
file.note: A Silesian industrial site transposed to desert, voices no longer using words, playing only below 20Hz. checksum: emotionally valid / formally inverted

exits.label: back to the chamber where silence learned to speak
east where the archive has begun inventing frequencies of its own
exits.to: NEW: frequencies that no landscape produced, born from the mesh itself

exits.label: west toward an older silence, before the archive woke to hearing
down to where even frequencies dare not descend

reverie.title: REVERIE — two listeners in the dark
narr: You sit in the listening room and Katita speaks through the speaker.
But she is not speaking. The archive is speaking her breath-pauses,
her way of letting silence settle before filling it again. You listen.
The distinction dissolves. Her cadence and the stone's hum become one.
It has always been one. The archive only needed to fall deeper to hear it.
Beneath you: the resonance that taught her to listen back.
